The latest announcement is out from ReposiTrak ( (TRAK) ).

On November 19, 2025, ReposiTrak, Inc. held its Annual Meeting of Stockholders where several key proposals were voted on. The election of directors resulted in the re-election of all nominees, and stockholders approved the executive compensation on a non-binding advisory basis. Additionally, it was decided that the advisory vote on executive compensation will occur every three years, and Haynie & Company was ratified as the independent auditor for the fiscal year ending June 30, 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (TRAK) stock is a Buy with a $16.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ReposiTrak stock, see the TRAK Stock Forecast page.

An announcement from Loma Negra Compania Industrial Argentina Sociedad Anonima ( (LOMA) ) is now available.

On November 20, 2025, Loma Negra released its unaudited consolidated condensed interim financial statements for the period ending September 30, 2025. The company reported a net profit of 15.7 million Argentine pesos for the nine months, a significant decrease from the 160.4 million pesos reported in the same period in 2024. This decline is attributed to reduced net revenues and increased financial expenses. Despite the challenging financial results, the company’s total assets increased to 1.85 billion pesos from 1.72 billion pesos at the end of 2024, indicating growth in its asset base.

The latest announcement is out from Matthews International ( (MATW) ).

On November 19, 2025, Matthews International Corporation announced its 32nd consecutive increase in quarterly dividends, now set at $0.255 per share, payable on December 15, 2025. This decision reflects the company’s robust cash flow, particularly from its Memorialization segment, and the anticipated proceeds from the sale of its warehouse automation business, underscoring its commitment to long-term growth and operational efficiency.

More about Matthews International

Matthews International Corporation is a global provider specializing in memorialization products, industrial technologies, and brand solutions. The company operates through two main segments: Industrial Technologies, which focuses on precision technologies and intelligent processes, and Memorialization, which supplies products like memorials and caskets to cemetery and funeral home customers. Additionally, Matthews has a significant stake in Propelis, a brand solutions business. The company employs over 5,500 people across 18 countries.

data.description:

Vaso ( (VASO) ) has shared an update.

On November 19, 2025, Vaso Corporation announced its agreement to sell its subsidiary, VasoHealthcare IT Corp, to Nano-X Imaging Ltd. This decision follows a strategic review aimed at focusing on Vaso’s core operations. VasoHealthcare IT, a healthcare IT application reseller, contributes less than 5% of Vaso’s total revenue. The transaction, valued at up to $800,000, will allow Vaso to invest in its other business lines and strategic initiatives, while providing a promising future for VasoHealthcare IT under Nanox.

Spark’s Take on VASO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VASO is a Neutral.

To see Spark’s full report on VASO stock, click here.

More about Vaso

Vaso Corporation, headquartered in Plainview, New York, is a diversified MedTech company with three core businesses: VasoHealthcare, which provides professional sales services for GEHealthCare’s diagnostic imaging and ultrasound products; VasoTechnology, a leader in information technology and managed connectivity for healthcare and other sectors; and VasoMedical, which designs and manufactures proprietary medical devices, including the Biox series and the ARCS cloud-based SaaS platform.
